name: Swayambhunath Stupa
description: Ancient hilltop stupa with iconic Buddha eyes. One of the most sacred Buddhist sites in Nepal. Origin legends date it to the creation of the Kathmandu Valley by Manjushri.

name: Boudhanath Stupa
description: One of the largest stupas in the world. Major center of Tibetan Buddhism in Nepal. Mandala-shaped with 13 rings representing the path to enlightenment.

name: Ashoka Stupa (Patan)
description: One of four stupas said to have been erected by Emperor Ashoka during his visit to the Kathmandu Valley in 249 BCE. Marks the southern boundary of ancient Patan.

name: Chilancho Stupa
description: Buddhist stupa in Kirtipur featuring beautifully carved stone panels depicting the life of Buddha. Partially damaged in the 2015 earthquake.

name: Charumati Vihara Stupa
description: Ancient stupa attributed to Princess Charumati (daughter of Emperor Ashoka). One of the oldest Buddhist sites in the Kathmandu Valley.

name: Mahaboudha Chaitya
description: Terracotta temple covered entirely with tiles bearing Buddha images. Inspired by the Mahabodhi Temple in Bodh Gaya. Located in a narrow courtyard in Patan.

name: Kathe Simbhu Stupa
description: Replica of Swayambhunath located in central Kathmandu. Built for devotees unable to climb to Swayambhunath. A popular urban Buddhist shrine.

name: Dhando Chaitya
description: Lichhavi-era stone chaitya with carved Buddha panels. One of the finest small chaityas in the Valley. Features detailed carvings on all four sides.
